title: Seele monoliths reverse sign
wikitext:
[]: Seele monoliths reverse sign             Quote Post by simon » Sun Aug 24, 2008 1:20 pm One little question. I'm in the middle of watching RoE and I saw something strange. 00:20:53 - Gendou is talking to Seele after the first battle in tokyo-3. We see the monoliths and there's a short scene of one monolith's back with a strange painting that looks like an owl. Later we see it on every monolith's back. I can't take a screen now coz of some embarrassing reasons. Anyway, I've never seen anything like that in the series. Any ideas?

[]: Re: Seele monoliths reverse sign             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:25 pm It's the new SEELE corporate logo. EDIT=== Added image [URL=http://img387.imageshack.us/my.php?image=seelenewscaledys1.jpg] [/URL]

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:34 pm No, you mean this: I have no idea what it is. Looks like a weird checkerboard to me, as well as a funky pentagram or something to me.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:36 pm Ummm... somekind of Pythagora's theorem, with some Hebrew in some parts. I think it represents some thing like that 'eye of the beholder+compass and setsquare' emblem of the Freemasons, or somekind of 'magical' emblem thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:36 pm Is that from the actual DVD release? Ugh I can't even make anything out... Maybe somebody could try to brighten up the image?

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:43 pm I think it was from online streaming...

[]:             Quote Post by cat42 » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:44 pm Lol, yay I'm retarded and can't remember the movie right.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Sun Aug 24, 2008 3:46 pm One thing I can say for sure, is that we can only give descriptions- nothing on its pupose, or significance...

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Aug 24, 2008 4:51 pm You guys must have short memories. Zuggy brought this up a while ago and correllated it to the Free Masons: http://www.masonicdictionary.com/47.html Screenshot from the DVD: http://www.tesuji.org/images/rebuild1_seele.png

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 24, 2008 5:03 pm Ornette: That DVD image is a LOT clearer. Thanks.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Sun Aug 24, 2008 5:08 pm The arrangement of the triangles makes it look like an owl. The upper one is the opened beak with two clearly visible eyes and two below are the wings. some owls have feathers in a chessboard pattern. An owl is a symbol of wisdom but there are other meanings that I can't find now.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Sun Aug 24, 2008 5:23 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Is that from the actual DVD release? Ugh I can't even make anything out... Maybe somebody could try to brighten up the image?

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sun Aug 24, 2008 6:03 pm It makes sense it would look like an owl due to the whole Lilith = Black Moon symbolism.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Aug 25, 2008 3:01 am If my research is correct, that pythagoras and those Hebrew may have got something to do with one of the first initiation rights you have to take to get into the Freemasons.... SO Anno and his minions have decided to go into the territory of the Freemasons....

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Mon Aug 25, 2008 4:26 am From urban dictionary, under definition of Lilith "Lilith's sacred animal is the Owl, symbolizing wisdom and learning." :>

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Mon Aug 25, 2008 5:01 pm The association between Lilith and owls has been popularized somewhat, though, for the record, it seems to be dubious on a scholarly basis. But, more to the point, the symbol on the monoliths really does not look anything like an owl. Ornette already posted a link to one of the elements. There's also an Eye of Providence located in the central triangle. The image -- and it's actually pretty rough and shitty-looking -- is printed in 'The Big Black Book', but you know how I am about scanning things in a timely fashion.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Aug 25, 2008 7:01 pm The Imperialist wrote: If my research is correct, that pythagoras and those Hebrew may have got something to do with one of the first initiation rights you have to take to get into the Freemasons.... SO Anno and his minions have decided to go into the territory of the Freemasons....

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Aug 26, 2008 9:11 am Yes, I think that is the whole point. BUt it is quite strange to bring up the Freemasons though... it looks EXACTLY like the symbol they use in their initiation rights, so maybe they are oging to put some extra plot line like, that Freemasons are actually a sub-organisation of SEELE or something like that.

[]:             Quote Post by simon » Tue Aug 26, 2008 11:22 am Soon we will find out that Seele are the mythical Knights Templar.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Tue Aug 26, 2008 5:39 pm if LAE is headed up by Hollywood, expect SEELE to be tied to either ^THAT^ or Freemasonry or the Illuminati, etc.
